# TruffleHog --exclude-paths regex allowlist (ADR-146).
#
# WHY THIS FILE EXISTS
# --------------------
# The Secret Scan gate runs TruffleHog with `--results=verified,unknown`
# (.github/workflows/ci.yml). For the Postgres detector an `unknown`
# result is emitted for ANY connection-string-shaped token — including
# the `postgres://` / `postgresql://` / `sslmode=` literals that
# connection-string-HANDLING code legitimately contains in error
# messages, doc comments, test names, and credential-free unit-test
# fixtures. The scanner cannot distinguish "code that parses DSNs" from
# "a leaked DSN", so the gate produces an irreducible false positive on
# the C4-OFF-04 PG-TLS work.
#
# POLICY (ADR-146): the sanctioned remediation is a MINIMALLY-SCOPED
# path exclusion — NOT weakening detectors and NOT dropping `unknown`
# globally. Each pattern below is anchored (`^...$`) to exactly ONE
# file that the C4-OFF-04 audit fix introduced/owns. The fixtures in
# these files carry NO credentials and use the RFC 6761 reserved,
# guaranteed-non-resolvable `.invalid` TLD, so excluding them removes
# zero real secret-detection coverage.
#
# Adding a path here REQUIRES: (1) the file genuinely handles
# connection strings as data, (2) it contains no real credentials,
# (3) an ADR-146-style rationale, (4) the narrowest possible anchored
# regex. See docs/adr/ADR-146-trufflehog-connection-string-fixture-allowlist.md
# and the C4-OFF-04 finding (ADR-128 transport-security addendum).
#
# One Go-RE2 regex per line; matched against the repo-relative path.

# C4-OFF-04: resolvePoolSsl() + the PG pool config. Contains
# `postgres://`/`postgresql://`/`sslmode=` tokens in error messages
# and the transport-security doc block — no secrets.
^src/indexer/postgres-store\.ts$

# C4-OFF-04: resolvePoolSsl() unit tests. DSNs are built from
# clearly-fake parts (host `db.invalid`, no userinfo) via a `dsn()`
# helper; the only credentialed literals are pre-existing green-main
# loopback (`127.0.0.1`) fixtures TruffleHog already does not flag.
^src/indexer/test/aud-128-postgres-store\.test\.ts$
